briefing.headline: Rig up a Lipless Crankbait — finesse post-spawn approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Lipless Crankbait is a strong choice for post-spawn with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: 'The bait HAS to hit something — rocks, grass, bottom. Simply retrieving in a straight line won't yield results. If the bait isn't hitting anything, stop reeling and let it sink until it does. Deflection and disturbance trigger strikes.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'The bait HAS to hit something — rocks, grass, bottom. Simply retrieving in a straight line won't yield results. If the bait isn't hitting anything, stop reeling and let it sink until it does. De
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, point, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'On shallow flats, use your rod tip and line size (10-20lb) to control depth. I want the bait hitting bottom. Every time it ticks cover, pop it and let it flutter — that's when fish bite.'
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, point, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: For lipless crankbaits in shallow grass (3-7ft): CC5 7'4" heavy action composite rod, 7.5:1 reel, 20lb fluoro. Multiple cranking rods with 14, 17, and 20lb line for different depths. Heavy line keeps the bait up and slows it down in shallow g...

Robertson: Spring crankbait progression -- 1) Prespawn: slow-roll lipless on flats near channel banks in 1-6 feet; 2) Postspawn: big-bodied squarebill on tapering points near spawning flats in under 10 feet; 3) Late postspawn: deep diver following...

Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal post-spawn fish hunker down at 6-12ft near cover. Males may temporarily leave fry. Focus on the deeper side of transition areas — channel edges, deeper points, and offshore structure near spawning flats.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
